symptoms.symptom_name: Severe bilateral ocular sicca/burning and xerostomia with diffuse arthralgia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral sicca with arthralgia on a known Sjogren's background is a systemic sicca flare.
Eye burning is non-specific; a corneal ulcer needs a slit-lamp to exclude.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden vision loss; symmetric bilateral eyes (not a single severe photophobic eye)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetric involvement without a single severe photophobic eye or vision loss is against an acute focal corneal ulcer, though a slit-lamp is needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever/unilateral hot tender parotid/pus (bacterial parotitis screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral non-tender enlargement without fever/pus argues against bacterial parotitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Can swallow liquids; dryness-related (not fixed obstruction) dysphagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to swallow liquids with no food impaction argues against a fixed obstruction; sicca explains the solid dysphagia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor caloric intake and corneal-integrity risk while isolated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to eat solids and overnight corneal exposure while alone risk malnutrition and corneal damage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral conjunctival injection/poor tear pooling; parched lobulated tongue; mild non-tender parotid enlargement; boggy MCP synovitis (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective sicca signs with parotid enlargement and symmetric synovitis confirm a Sjogren's flare in person.
Objective exocrinopathy/synovitis argues against a purely subjective pain syndrome.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A severe bilateral sicca flare with symmetric synovitis, no sudden vision loss/single severe eye, no febrile parotid swelling, and preserved liquid swallowing, is a systemic Sjogren's flare — but the cornea can't be examined remotely (corneal ulcer risk) so urgent optometry/ophthalmology review plus intensive lubrication and nutrition support are required, with emergency eye care for any ocular red flag.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess corneal integrity and exclude ulceration; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person GP review and bloods (inflammatory markers, review of immunomodulators)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess the systemic flare and optimise therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Parotid assessment/imaging if a unilateral tender swelling develops
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if bacterial parotitis features appear.
